name: Moveworks
description: Moveworks is an enterprise AI platform acquired by ServiceNow in November 2024 for ~$1.5B. Now integrated as ServiceNow EmployeeWorks, it combines Moveworks conversational AI and enterprise search with ServiceNow's unified portal and autonomous workflows. In February 2026, ServiceNow launched Autonomous Workforce with Moveworks — AI specialists that autonomously diagnose and resolve IT support requests, achieving 99% faster resolution than human agents.
oneLiner: Enterprise agentic AI platform for cross-department employee support and workflow automation, now part of ServiceNow.
funding: Acquired by ServiceNow (~$1.5B, Nov 2024). Previously raised $305M total.
pricing: Enterprise-only, per-employee/month (~$3-8/employee). No self-serve or free tier.
capabilities: Natural language IT service desk automation
Cross-department support (IT, HR, Finance, Facilities, Legal)
Conversational AI via Slack, Teams, email, web

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Agent Studio visual no-code builder: select templates or start from scratch, define triggers, configure connected systems, set up flows, establish approval/escalation rules. Creator Studio extends with API-first for custom integrations. Targets IT admins and operations, not developers.
strengths: 100+ native enterprise connectors with deep read/write
60%+ autonomous IT ticket resolution at scale
NL interface via Slack/Teams — meets employees where they work
ServiceNow acquisition provides unmatched stability and distribution
Purpose-built for enterprise support with strong out-of-box value
weaknesses: Agent Studio is config tool more than visual programming
Closed ecosystem, no MCP, limited open standards
Pricing opacity — enterprise-only, no self-serve evaluation
Weak multi-agent — routing not true collaboration
Developer experience secondary — no Git workflows, limited debugging
notableFeatures: MoveLM — proprietary LLM fine-tuned on hundreds of millions of enterprise interactions
Agent Studio no-code builder with templates
Creator Studio API platform for custom workflows
Reasoning Engine for multi-step agentic orchestration

recentDevelopments: 2026-02: ServiceNow launched Autonomous Workforce — AI specialists that execute jobs with enterprise governance
2026-02: ServiceNow EmployeeWorks GA — combines Moveworks conversational AI with ServiceNow portal for 200M employees
2026-02: L1 Service Desk AI Specialist — resolves IT cases 99% faster than human agents (controlled availability)
2026: Scoped Assistants, no-code Assistant Builder, and Headless API for deployment anywhere

deploymentOptions.notes: Cloud SaaS (now part of ServiceNow). Hybrid integration with on-prem IT systems via connectors.

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: Agent Studio no-code visual builder. NL descriptions scaffold logic. Templates. Under 30 min for templated agents.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Conversation analytics dashboard. Basic simulated testing. No advanced trace or regression testing.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: Setup guides, API refs, Moveworks Academy. More sales-oriented than developer-oriented.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Agent Studio config changes without redeployment. Creator Studio needs more effort.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: Multi-skill routing across IT/HR/Finance. Reasoning Engine sequential. Not complex topologies.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: Conversational handoff with context. Manager approval. No Board Operator-style dashboards.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: Agent Studio team admin. No Git-native, collaborative editing, or CI/CD for agents.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: Pre-built templates for IT, HR, facilities. Curated by Moveworks, not community marketplace.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: 100+ native: ServiceNow, Jira, Okta, Workday, SAP, Salesforce, Slack, Teams. Deep read/write.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: REST API for Creator Studio. Webhooks. No MCP, GraphQL, gRPC. Proprietary framework.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: Enterprise OAuth, SAML, SSO. Per-agent role-based permissions. Credential management.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: Enterprise KB crawling. Semantic index. Handles common doc formats. Purpose-built enterprise RAG.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: Agents pausable/disableable. Guardrails prevent out-of-scope. Input/output filtering. Topic-level control.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: Approval workflows for provisioning/access. Manager routing. Functional, not sophisticated.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: SOC 2 Type II. Comprehensive logs. GDPR data residency. HIPAA-ready. Queryable and exportable.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: Role-based admin. Team hierarchy. SSO. Per-agent permission scoping by org structure.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: SaaS-delivered. Moveworks manages infra. Limited self-hosted. ServiceNow may expand.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: Enterprise-scale: 50K+ employee companies, thousands of daily requests. Millions of interactions.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Performance insights: resolution rates, response times, satisfaction, topic trends.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: 500+ enterprise customers: Broadcom, Hearst, Palo Alto Networks, Databricks. Gartner/Forrester recognized.

scoringRationale: 70/100 overall. Strongest: Integration (16/20). Weakest: Orchestration (11/20). Breakdown: Creation (13/20), Orchestration (11/20), Integration (16/20), Governance (15/20), Operations (15/20).
targetUser: IT and HR teams automating employee support at scale

communityBuzz: ServiceNow acquisition (.5B) seen as validation of enterprise agent category
60%+ autonomous IT ticket resolution rate frequently cited as benchmark
Post-acquisition roadmap uncertainty — will Moveworks maintain identity under ServiceNow?
Pricing opacity criticized — enterprise-only with no self-serve evaluation path

metrics.value: Leader in Conversational AI for Employee Services

walkthrough.title: How Moveworks Works: Enterprise Agent Studio and Marketplace

steps.title: Enterprise Agent Platform
steps.description: Homepage — Find answers, Automate tasks, Build AI agents. Company email required to access — enterprise sales motion from the start.

works: Clear value prop in one line — three use cases, no ambiguity
Conference-on-demand section builds credibility and FOMO
Clean design with clear CTA — Get started button is prominent
doesntWork: Mandatory email + company name before seeing anything — maximum friction
No product screenshots or demo video — users must trust marketing copy
'Get started' implies immediate access but starts a sales process
pmCritique.userPsychology: The gated landing is a deliberate filter — Moveworks wants enterprise buyers, not tire-kickers. Every form field is a qualification signal.

steps.title: Agent Studio Overview
steps.description: Build Intelligent AI agents, faster — three pillars: For all teams (IT/HR/Sales), For all systems (CRMs, ERPs), For all employees.

works: Three-pillar messaging covers every enterprise buying objection
Get a demo + Try now dual CTAs serve cautious and eager buyers
Cross-department positioning differentiates from vertical-only tools
doesntWork: No actual product UI shown — marketing messaging, not demonstration
'Faster' without a benchmark — faster than what?
No pricing signals — can't estimate budget before engaging sales
pmCritique.userPsychology: Agent Studio's page sells the vision, not the product. Enterprise buyers expect this — they buy outcomes, not features. Developer evaluators leave disappointed.

steps.title: 110+ Integrations
steps.description: Developer section — Agentic Automation Engine, Content Gateway, AI Agent Marketplace. Featured integrations: Jira, Salesforce, ServiceNow.

works: 110+ integrations signals enterprise-grade connectivity depth
Featured integrations (Jira, Salesforce, ServiceNow) are exactly what buyers look for
Developer portal with Documentation, Academy, Community shows ecosystem investment
doesntWork: Marketplace preview too small to read — requires clicking through
Explore Marketplace leads to another marketing page, not a live catalog
No API-first positioning — developers must go through the Marketplace
pmCritique.userPsychology: The integration ecosystem is Moveworks' real moat — once connected to Jira + Salesforce + ServiceNow, switching costs are enormous.

steps.title: AI Agent Marketplace
steps.description: Pre-built agents with 110+ integration icons — Notion, Slack, GitHub, Zoom, Datadog. Install, configure, deploy in minutes.

works: Logo grid is visually impressive — recognizable brands build instant trust
Explore Marketplace + View All Agents serve browsing and deep-dive modes
App store mental model — familiar pattern for installing capabilities
doesntWork: No agent descriptions visible — logos don't explain what each agent does
No ratings or usage counts — can't tell production-tested from experimental
Generic marketing copy — no specific outcome promises per agent
pmCritique.userPsychology: The Marketplace is Moveworks' answer to 'can it work with our tools?' The logo wall closes deals that product demos alone can't.

steps.title: Workflow Automation
steps.description: Agentic Automation Engine — build cross-department workflows that connect multiple systems and trigger actions automatically.

works: Cross-department automation is the killer use case — IT + HR + Finance in one flow
Agentic framing means workflows can make decisions, not just move data
Enterprise-grade means audit trails, approval chains, compliance built in
doesntWork: No visual workflow builder shown — only marketing description
Workflow complexity hidden behind 'agentic' buzzword
No comparison to existing automation tools (Workato, Zapier, Power Automate)
pmCritique.userPsychology: Workflow automation is where Moveworks justifies its enterprise pricing — connecting SAP to ServiceNow to Slack in one agentic flow is genuinely hard and genuinely valuable.

steps.title: Analytics and Monitoring
steps.description: Track agent performance — resolution rates, response times, user satisfaction. Data-driven agent improvement over time.

works: Performance metrics turn agents from experiments into managed services
Resolution rates quantify ROI — essential for budget renewal conversations
Monitoring enables continuous improvement — agents get better over time
doesntWork: Analytics dashboard not shown in video — only described conceptually
No real-time alerting visible — reactive monitoring rather than proactive
Metrics definition unclear — what counts as 'resolved'?
pmCritique.userPsychology: Analytics is what separates enterprise agent platforms from open-source frameworks. The ability to prove ROI in a dashboard is worth the entire Moveworks premium.

steps.title: Enterprise Deployment
steps.description: Deploy agents across Slack, Teams, email, web portals, and service desks — meet employees where they already work.

works: Multi-channel deployment — Slack, Teams, email, web from one platform
Meet users where they work — no new app to install or learn
Enterprise channels mean IT-approved, security-reviewed, compliance-ready
doesntWork: Deployment options are described, not shown — no configuration walkthrough
Channel-specific behavior customization isn't visible
No self-service deployment — likely requires professional services engagement
pmCritique.userPsychology: Multi-channel deployment is Moveworks' enterprise superpower — the agent works in Slack for engineering, Teams for sales, email for executives. One agent, everywhere.

pmVerdict.summary: Moveworks (now ServiceNow EmployeeWorks) is the enterprise agent platform that's hardest to evaluate — fully gated behind sales, no self-serve trial, no public product demos. The 110+ integration marketplace and cross-department positioning (IT/HR/Sales/Finance) are compelling. But the acquisition by ServiceNow creates uncertainty: is this still Moveworks or just a ServiceNow feature? The PM verdict: strong vision, impossible to verify without vendor engagement.
strengths: Cross-department positioning (IT + HR + Sales + Finance) in one platform
Marketplace model makes agent deployment feel like installing apps
ServiceNow backing provides enterprise credibility and distribution
risks: Zero self-serve evaluation path — must engage sales to see the product
Post-acquisition identity crisis — Moveworks vs ServiceNow EmployeeWorks
No public product demos or screenshots of actual agent building
Community and developer ecosystem unclear post-acquisition

ctoVerdict.summary: Moveworks has the strongest enterprise integration story (110+ connectors, ServiceNow-native) but the complete lack of self-serve evaluation is a red flag. Post-ServiceNow acquisition, the platform's independence and roadmap are uncertain. If you're already a ServiceNow shop, this is the natural choice. If not, the lock-in is extreme.
strengths: ServiceNow integration is deepest in the market — native, not bolted on
Enterprise-grade compliance and security via ServiceNow platform
Proven at scale — $2.85B acquisition validates enterprise traction
dealBreakers: No self-serve trial or sandbox — 3-6 month sales cycle minimum
Pricing opaque — enterprise-only, no published plans
Post-acquisition roadmap uncertainty — features may be absorbed into ServiceNow

coverageGaps: Actual product UI and agent building experience

scoringAlignment.videoEvidence: Only marketing pages shown — no actual builder UI demonstrated
scoringAlignment.gap: Agent Studio claimed but never seen in any public content

scoringAlignment.videoEvidence: Cross-department workflows described but not shown
scoringAlignment.gap: Orchestration capabilities entirely based on marketing claims

scoringAlignment.videoEvidence: 110+ integration logos visible in marketplace page
scoringAlignment.gap: Logo presence ≠ integration depth — no connector configuration shown

scoringAlignment.videoEvidence: ServiceNow platform governance assumed but not demonstrated
scoringAlignment.gap: Governance score relies on ServiceNow platform inheritance

scoringAlignment.videoEvidence: No deployment, monitoring, or scaling demonstrated
scoringAlignment.gap: Operations score entirely based on ServiceNow platform capabilities

youtubeReviews.title: Moveworks Tutorial for Beginners | Moveworks AI Assistant Demo
How Moveworks Went From First Customer to $2.85B

coreCompetence.bestAt: The deepest enterprise IT support AI — now part of ServiceNow, it has the most mature understanding of IT workflows, ticket routing, and employee experience automation.
coreCompetence.targetUser: Large enterprise IT departments (5000+ employees) looking to automate tier-1 IT support, HR inquiries, and internal help desk across multiple systems.
coreCompetence.vsAlternatives: Wins against all competitors on enterprise IT domain depth and ServiceNow integration. Wins on deployment track record (Fortune 500). Loses on flexibility — narrow to IT/HR use cases. Loses to general agent platforms on breadth of application.
